# Appendix C. Portable Command Cheat Sheet {#sec-cheatsheet .unnumbered}

A quick reference for the commands this book uses most. Nothing here
is new: each command gets its real introduction, with live output, in
the chapter noted beside it, and this page is the map. See
@sec-command-anatomy for how to read the flags and arguments below, and
the [divergence reference](#sec-divergence) for the commands marked
**DIV**, whose behavior differs across macOS and Linux.

## Navigating and inspecting files {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 5](chapters/05-navigation.qmd) (first met in
[Chapter 2](chapters/02-mental-model.qmd)).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `pwd` | print the working directory | 5 |
| `cd` | change directory (`cd -` returns to previous) | 5 |
| `ls` | list a directory (`-l` long, `-a` all, `-R` recursive); **DIV** `-G`, `total` | 5 |
| `cat` | print a file, or concatenate several | 5 |
| `less` | page through a file (`q` to quit) | 4 |
| `head` / `tail` | first / last N lines (`-n`; `tail -f` follows) | 7 |
| `tree` | directory as an indented outline (or `ls -R`) | 5 |
| `stat` | full file metadata; **DIV** `-c` vs `-f` | 5 |
| `wc` | count lines, words, bytes (`-l`, `-c`) | 2 |

## Manipulating files and directories {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 5](chapters/05-navigation.qmd);
`chmod`/`chown` in [Chapter 10](chapters/10-processes-permissions-secrets.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `cp` | copy (`-r` recursive, `-i` prompt); silent overwrite | 5 |
| `mv` | move or rename (`-i` prompt) | 5 |
| `rm` | remove; no undo (`-r`, `-f`, `-i`); guard with `${VAR:?}` | 7 |
| `mkdir` | make a directory (`-p` parents; `{a,b}` braces) | 5 |
| `touch` | create an empty file, or update mtime (`-t`; **DIV** `-d`) | 5 |
| `ln -s` | create a symbolic link (target, then link) | 5 |
| `chmod` | change permissions (`600`, `644`, `755`; `u+rw`) | 10 |
| `chown` / `chgrp` | change owner / group | 10 |

## Pipes, redirection, and streams {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 7](chapters/07-pipes-redirection-danger.qmd)
(`tee` is met in [Chapter 11](chapters/11-scripts-fail-loudly.qmd)).

```bash
cmd1 | cmd2       # pipe stdout of cmd1 into cmd2
cmd > file        # redirect stdout, truncating file
cmd >> file       # redirect stdout, appending
cmd 2> file       # redirect stderr
cmd > f 2>&1      # merge stderr into stdout, then to f
set -o noclobber  # make > refuse to overwrite (>| forces)
cmd | tee run.log # write to a file and the screen at once
find . -print0 | xargs -0 cmd  # space-safe file list
```

## Text and tabular data {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 8](chapters/08-text-tabular-data.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `grep` | print lines matching a pattern (`-n`, `-c`, `-F`, `-q`) | 8 |
| `sed` | edit a stream (`s/old/new/`); **DIV** `-i` (use `-i.bak`) | 8 |
| `awk` | filter and compute on fields (`-F,`, `$1`, `NR>1`); **DIV** | 8 |
| `sort` / `uniq` | sort lines / count adjacent dups (`sort` piped to `uniq -c`); **DIV** locale | 8 |
| `cut` / `tr` | pick columns (`-d`, `-f`) / translate chars (`-d '\r'`) | 8 |
| `jq` | query JSON (`.a.b`, `keys`, `-r`) | 8 |
| `duckdb` | SQL over CSV/Parquet (`-csv`, `-c`, `DESCRIBE`) | 8 |

## Finding things {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 9](chapters/09-finding-things.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `find` | walk the tree by name/type/size/time; **DIV** `-regex`, `-printf` | 9 |
| `fd` | ergonomic `find` (`-e` ext); not on bare servers | 9 |
| `rg` (ripgrep) | search file contents across a tree (`-n`, `-g`, `-l`) | 9 |

`rg` and `fd` skip `.gitignore`d paths (often your `data/`) by default:
add `--no-ignore` when that matters.

## Processes, jobs, permissions, secrets {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 10](chapters/10-processes-permissions-secrets.qmd).

| Item | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `ps` | report on processes (`aux`, `-o pid,args`); **DIV** | 10 |
| `&` / `jobs` / `$!` | run in background / list jobs / last job's PID | 10 |
| `kill` | send a signal (default TERM; `-9` KILL, no cleanup) | 10 |
| `nohup ... &` | keep a job alive past a hangup | 10 |
| `export` | put a variable in the environment (secrets, not argv) | 10 |
| history hygiene | leading space; `HISTCONTROL` / `HIST_IGNORE_SPACE`; **DIV** | 10 |
| `chmod 600` + `.gitignore` | lock down `.env` / keys (two halves of one habit) | 10 |

## Scripting and safety {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 11](chapters/11-scripts-fail-loudly.qmd).

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash     # the standard reproducible
set -euo pipefail       #   script header: pick the
export LC_ALL=C         #   interpreter, fail loudly,
export TZ=UTC           #   pin locale and timezone
```

| Item | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `[[ ... ]]` | bash test (`-f`, `-d`, `-z`, `-gt`, `==`) | 11 |
| `$?` / `exit` | read / set the exit code (0 = success) | 11 |
| `if` / `for` / `while` | branch and loop; `while IFS= read -r` | 11 |
| `trap '...' ERR` | run a handler when a command fails | 11 |
| `shellcheck` | lint a script (flags `SC2086`, unquoted vars); **DIV** none | 11 |
| `shfmt -d` / `-w` | format a script (preview / write) | 11 |
| `bash -x` | trace execution to find the failing line | 11 |
| `/usr/bin/time` | time and peak memory; **DIV** `-v` vs `-l` | 11 |
| `sha256sum` / `shasum -a 256` | checksum and verify (`-c`); **DIV** name | 3, 11 |

## Build and environments {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 12](chapters/12-make-pipeline.qmd) and
[Chapter 13](chapters/13-environments.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `make` | rebuild only what changed (TAB-indented recipes); `make check` | 12 |
| `make -n` / `-B` | preview without running / force a full rebuild | 12 |
| `uv sync` / `run` / `add` | restore / run inside / change the Python env | 13 |
| `renv::restore()` / `snapshot()` | restore / record the R env | 13 |
| `Rscript -e` / `quarto render` | run R non-interactively / render the report | 12 |

## Remote and sessions {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 14](chapters/14-ssh-remote-compute.qmd) and
[Chapter 15](chapters/15-persistent-sessions.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `ssh` | shell on a remote machine (`host 'cmd'`, `-L`, `~/.ssh/config`) | 14 |
| `ssh-keygen` / `ssh-add` | make/inspect a keypair / load it into the agent | 14 |
| `scp` / `rsync` | copy one file / sync trees (`-avn`, `--delete`); **DIV** | 14 |
| `tar` | bundle a tree (`-czf` create, `-xzf` extract) | 14 |
| `du` / `df` / `ncdu` | tree usage / free space / interactive usage | 14 |
| `tmux` | persistent, detachable sessions (`new -s`, `attach -t`, `Ctrl-b d`) | 15 |

## Help and discovery {.unnumbered}

See [Chapter 2](chapters/02-mental-model.qmd).

| Command | What it does | Ch |
|---|---|---|
| `man` | the full manual page (authority for your system) | 2 |
| `--help` | quick usage summary; **DIV** (a GNU habit) | 2 |
| `type -a` | how the shell interprets a name (all matches) | 2 |
| `command -v` | which file actually runs (POSIX; the trustworthy one) | 2 |
| `tldr` | community worked examples | 2 |

See the [reproducibility and safety checklist](#sec-checklist) for the
pass that ties these commands together before you circulate results.
